skill.name: developing-genkit-js
skill.description: Develop AI-powered applications using Genkit in Node.js/TypeScript. Use when the user asks about Genkit, AI agents, flows, or tools in JavaScript/TypeScript, or when encountering Genkit errors, validation issues, type errors, or API problems.
skill.summary: Reference guide for building AI applications with Genkit in JavaScript and TypeScript projects.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: All 194 static analyzer findings are false positives. The skill consists entirely of markdown documentation and code examples for the Genkit JS framework. The 176 'external_commands' detections are markdown inline code spans (backtick formatting) showing CLI commands like `genkit --version` and `npx tsc --noEmit`, not actual shell execution. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings have no corresponding crypto operations in the source. The 'hardcoded URL' findings are documentation references (localhost dev UI and example.com placeholder). The 'env_access' finding is documentation instructing users to set API keys. No executable code exists in this skill. Safe to publish.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Code Examples
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 176 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' across all markdown files. These are all markdown inline code formatting (e.g., `genkit --version`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npm install -g genkit-cli`). No actual shell execution or code injection exists. The skill contains only documentation text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged hardcoded URLs at references/docs-and-cli.md:21 (http://localhost:4000, the Genkit Dev UI localhost address) and references/examples.md:128 (https://example.com/photo.jpg, a placeholder example URL). These are documentation references, not actual network requests.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Environment Variable Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Generic API/secret keys' at references/setup.md:40. This line instructs users to set environment variables like GEMINI_API_KEY. It is documentation guidance, not actual credential access or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at multiple locations in SKILL.md, references/best-practices.md, references/common-errors.md, and references/examples.md. No cryptographic operations exist in any file. These are likely false matches on model names or configuration strings in documentation text.

content.user_title: Build AI apps with Genkit in JavaScript
content.value_statement: Developers need guidance to use the Genkit framework correctly in Node.js and TypeScript projects. This skill provides reference documentation, common error solutions, and code patterns for building AI-powered applications with Genkit.
actual_capabilities: Guide Genkit project setup with correct package imports and configuration
Troubleshoot Genkit v1.x migration issues including deprecated API patterns
Generate code for text generation, structured output, and streaming flows
Configure multimodal features including image generation and speech synthesis
Use Genkit CLI commands for documentation lookup, flow execution, and evaluation
Apply best practices for schema definitions, flow design, and project structure
limitations: Provides documentation guidance only; does not execute Genkit flows or test code
Model recommendations focus on Google AI; other providers require manual configuration
Examples use specific Gemini model versions that may require updates over time
Does not replace official Genkit documentation or support channels

use_cases.title: Set up a new Genkit project
use_cases.description: A developer wants to add AI features to an existing Node.js application using Genkit. The skill guides directory structure, package installation, plugin configuration, and initial flow creation.

use_cases.title: Migrate from Genkit pre-1.0 to v1.x
use_cases.description: A team maintains a Genkit application built before the v1.0 release. The skill identifies deprecated APIs, provides migration patterns for imports, model references, and flow definitions.

use_cases.title: Build multimodal AI features
use_cases.description: A product team wants to add image generation or text-to-speech to their application. The skill provides working examples for Gemini multimodal models with correct configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Set up Genkit in a new project
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to add AI features to my Node.js project using Genkit. Help me set up the project structure, install the required packages, and create a simple flow that generates text.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: First-time Genkit user starting a new project

prompt_templates.title: Fix Genkit type errors
prompt_templates.prompt: I am getting TypeScript errors in my Genkit flow. Check the common errors reference and help me identify if this is a deprecated API issue or an import problem.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Debugging type errors after Genkit v1.x migration

prompt_templates.title: Create a structured output flow
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a Genkit flow that returns structured JSON output using Zod schemas. Show me how to define the schema, create the flow, and access the typed output.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Building flows with structured data output

prompt_templates.title: Add image generation with streaming
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to build a Genkit flow that generates images using Gemini 2.5 Flash Image model and streams partial results. Include the correct responseModalities configuration and explain how to handle the media output.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Multimodal flow with proper configuration

output_examples.input: Set up a new Genkit project with Google AI provider
output: Create the ai directory in your source folder
Create genkit.ts with genkit() initialization and googleAI plugin
Create flows and tools subdirectories for organization
Add genkit:ui script to package.json for Dev UI access

output_examples.input: Fix ValidationError when using defineFlow with pre-1.0 imports
output: Replace `import { defineFlow } from "@genkit-ai/flow"` with defining flows on the ai instance
Replace `import { genkit } from "@genkit-ai/core"` with `import { genkit } from "genkit"`
Access response text via `response.text` property instead of `response.text()` method
Run type check with `npx tsc --noEmit` to verify the migration is complete

best_practices: Always use the Genkit CLI (`genkit docs:search`, `genkit docs:read`) to look up current documentation rather than relying on internal knowledge, as Genkit APIs change frequently between versions.
Store API keys and sensitive configuration in environment variables or .env files. Never hardcode credentials in source files or commit them to version control.

Use Zod schemas from the `genkit` package (not the standalone `zod` package) for all input and output definitions. Add `.describe()` annotations to guide the LLM in populating schema fields correctly.
anti_patterns: Do not import from deprecated packages like `@genkit-ai/core`, `@genkit-ai/ai`, or `@genkit-ai/flow`. Import core functionality directly from the `genkit` package instead.
Do not skip reading the common errors reference when troubleshooting. Many errors are caused by deprecated API usage that has documented solutions.
Do not use decommissioned model versions like `gemini-2.0-*` or `gemini-1.5-*`. Always use the latest generation models specified in the best practices reference.

faq.question: What is Genkit and what does this skill do?
faq.answer: Genkit is a framework for building AI-powered applications in JavaScript and TypeScript. This skill provides reference documentation, common error solutions, and code patterns to help you develop with Genkit correctly.

faq.question: Which AI model providers does Genkit support?
faq.answer: Genkit supports multiple providers including Google AI, OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama. This skill defaults to Google AI examples, but you can search the docs with `genkit docs:search plugins` to find configuration for other providers.

faq.question: What is the minimum Genkit CLI version required?
faq.answer: The minimum required CLI version is 1.29.0. You can verify your version with `genkit --version` and install or upgrade with `npm install -g genkit-cli@^1.29.0`.

faq.question: How do I troubleshoot Genkit errors?
faq.answer: Always read the common errors reference first. Most errors are caused by deprecated pre-1.0 API usage. Match your error to the documented patterns and apply the solutions before searching external sources.

faq.question: How do I enable the Genkit Developer UI?
faq.answer: Run your application with `genkit start -- <your-start-command>`. This enables the Developer UI, typically available at http://localhost:4000, where you can test flows and monitor execution.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Next.js or Firebase projects?
faq.answer: Yes. Genkit integrates with Next.js (via @genkit-ai/next) and Firebase (via @genkit-ai/firebase). The skill detects your framework from package.json and adapts its guidance accordingly.
